mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS has positive 3-month momentum, stronger household spending raises near-term revenue expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports RSAFS at 352138.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum does not produce higher mean 20-day SPX returns than negative momentum in walk-forward tests, or if hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS crossing above 4.5% or rising more than 50 bp in 3 months tightens discount rates and credit conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% or rises > 0.50pp in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports FEDFUNDS at 2.5 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases or if triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth combined with a falling UNRATE identifies broad labor-income strength; the joint signal increases earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports PAYEMS at 132843.0 and UNRATE at 5.4 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down trigger has no positive excess 20-day return over either single-series signal in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp INDPRO contraction signals weakening production and operating leverage; when industrial production falls more than 1% over 3 months, S&P 500 returns are negative over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports INDPRO at 117.667 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by nonnegative average 60-day SPX returns or if downside hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL acceleration paired with rising FEDFUNDS identifies inflation-driven tightening; the joint pressure lowers equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation rate rises and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25pp
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports CPIAUCSL at 191.3 and FEDFUNDS at 2.5
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI acceleration plus FEDFUNDS rise interaction is not more negative for 20-day SPX returns than either CPIAUCSL acceleration or FEDFUNDS increases alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST above its 12-month average signals credit-sensitive housing demand and durable-goods spillovers; this predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 12-month moving average and its 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports HOUST at 2159.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST cross-above signals are followed by lower 60-day SPX returns than non-triggered periods or if the estimated coefficient is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE increase greater than 0.3 percentage point in 3 months signals rising recession risk and weaker earnings; it predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports UNRATE at 5.4 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise triggers produce positive average 60-day SPX returns or fail to reduce returns relative to periods with stable or falling UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: GDPC1 growth together with rising INDPRO identifies synchronized real activity expansion; the interaction raises earnings breadth and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth > 0 and INDPRO 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports GDPC1 at 10993.3 and INDPRO at 117.667
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-positive and INDPRO-positive signal has a 5-day SPX hit rate below 50% or no positive excess return versus unconditional returns.
